meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn a professional client-centric workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to turn real-world problems into portfolio pieces.
content.answer_block: Stop building 'to-do' lists and start solving real-world friction. By adopting a client-centric workflow, you transform ambiguous ideas into high-value SaaS tools. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to iterate rapidly, ensuring every line of code you write solves a tangible business problem for a hypothetical client.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the silent assassin of developer momentum. You have the skills, you have the stack, but you lack the 'why.' Most students fall into the trap of cloning generic tutorials, leaving them with a portfolio that blends into the noise of a thousand identical CRUD apps.

True engineering isn't just about syntax; it's about the translation of human pain into technical solutions. Today, we shift your mindset from 'student coder' to 'solution architect.' By adopting a professional client workflow, you’ll stop searching for ideas and start uncovering them through structured discovery, turning every project into a case study that screams 'hire me.'

steps.title: Define the Friction Point
steps.description: Identify a repetitive task performed by a local business or niche community. Focus on data entry or manual reporting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Manager. Analyze the workflow of a local independent coffee shop. Identify 3 manual tasks that could be automated via a web-based dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a PRD for an automated inventory tracking system for small cafes.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Model
steps.description: Map the entities required to solve the problem. A robust schema is the backbone of any professional application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for an inventory management system. Include tables for products, suppliers, and transaction logs. Use Prisma syntax.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a schema.prisma file based on the PRD generated in step 1.'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Core
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate, ensuring clean separation of concerns from the start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js application with TypeScript and Tailwind. Integrate the Prisma schema and set up a basic API route for fetching inventory data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure and install all necessary dependencies.'

steps.title: Implement Critical Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'Unique Value Proposition'—the one feature that actually solves the client's biggest pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure server action for updating inventory levels. Include error handling and validation logic using Zod.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the updateInventory server action and add unit tests to verify data integrity.'

steps.title: Polished Interface Integration
steps.description: Build a clean, functional UI that prioritizes user experience over aesthetic fluff.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard component using Shadcn UI. It must display the inventory list and provide a simple form for stock adjustments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the main dashboard page and link it to the server actions.'

pro_tips: Always build with 'audit logs' in mind; clients value data integrity over fancy animations.
Use mock data factories to test edge cases before you even touch the frontend.
Document your architectural decisions in a README.md; employers look for the 'why' behind your code.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves 80% of the problem rather than building a bloated feature set.
Treat your code as a product; iterate based on feedback loops, not just your initial assumption.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel, record a 60-second video demo explaining the business problem you solved, and add it to your LinkedIn portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to move from a tutorial-follower to a product-builder. Every project you complete this way adds real value to your resume and your confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
